sourceMovie.title: Beverly Hills Cop
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Beverly Hills Cop (1984) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: 21 Jump Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Beverly Hills Cop clicked for you, 21 Jump Street (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Beverly Hills Cop clicked for you, Hot Fuzz (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Beverly Hills Cop?
faq.answer: Try 21 Jump Street, Hot Fuzz, Bad Boys—each shares DNA with Beverly Hills Cop while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Beverly Hills Cop?
faq.answer: Beverly Hills Cop (1984) is best described as Comedy, Crime, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Beverly Hills Cop part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Beverly Hills Cop Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
